data.narrative:
Ulysses Ecosystem Engineering is a Series A-stage company operating in Ocean & Maritime technology, founded by Colm O'Brien, Will O'Brien, Akhil Voorakkara, and Jamie Wedderburn. The company has raised $9.55M to date. The comparable universe for this frontier subsector is limited, consisting of two identifiable private peers (Bedrock Ocean and Havoc AI) and no direct public comparables.

Valuation analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, the primary applicable triangulation approach given data constraints. Using total capital raised of $9.55M as a Series A baseline and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of progression to the next funding stage yields an implied valuation of $19.1M. This reflects normalized venture cadence assumptions rather than sector-specific multiples, which are difficult to establish with a two-company peer set.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $16M to $22M, with a median of $19M. The range reflects reasonable variance around stage-typical step-ups (1.7x to 2.3x multipliers), absent richer comparable data or revenue metrics. Key uncertainties include the traction profile relative to Bedrock Ocean and Havoc AI, capital efficiency trends within ocean tech, and whether frontier-stage risk commands a discount to standard Series A progression assumptions. Additional metrics on revenue run-rate, unit economics, or customer concentration would sharpen conviction in this range.
